title: Yellow Subterfuge

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy but zero originality at this point in the series

jokes.text: Skinner fantasy musical sequence with increasingly absurd lyrics
jokes.setup: Skinner daydreaming about being cool and respected
jokes.punchline: The fantasy escalates to making Bart spill his milk and looking good in chaps
jokes.explanation: Creative way to show Skinner's delusions of grandeur through musical fantasy - good character work

jokes.text: Wake up. You're on!
jokes.punchline: Abrupt return to reality - Skinner was just daydreaming
jokes.explanation: Clean transition back to reality with good economy

jokes.text: sign here stickers came from last year's field trip to the sign here sticker factory
jokes.setup: Skinner mentions using special stickers on permission slips
jokes.punchline: The circular logic that the stickers came from a field trip to make those very stickers
jokes.explanation: Clever recursive joke with absurdist logic that's both original and well-structured

jokes.text: my old navy buddy-- we both love to shop at Old Navy
jokes.punchline: Clarifies they're shopping buddies at the Old Navy store, not military navy
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection playing on navy/Navy wordplay with good economy

jokes.text: So you're all excited, eh? Yay! Well, that's too bad, because many of you will not be going.
jokes.setup: Students get excited about submarine trip
jokes.punchline: Skinner immediately crushes their hopes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - builds hope then destroys it, excellent character integration

jokes.text: Whoo-hoo! Well, I get to go!
jokes.setup: Bart assumes he's automatically included despite being the worst behaved
jokes.punchline: His immediate confidence despite obvious reality
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart obliviousness - good character work but predictable

jokes.text: I am the law. I hold the red pen.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining his authority over the list
jokes.punchline: Grandiose declaration about wielding a red pen
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Skinner's power trip with memorable phrasing

jokes.text: That's what pens do.
jokes.setup: Explaining that once crossed off, you stay crossed off
jokes.punchline: Deadpan observation about the basic function of pens
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery of obvious fact presented as profound wisdom - very quotable

jokes.text: Help! I'm trapped in space with a man I don't like!
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration while playing alone
jokes.explanation: Decent child play dialogue but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Die, Nazi octopus, die!
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous concept of a Nazi octopus as an enemy
jokes.explanation: Absurd juxtaposition of historical evil with sea creature - good economy and quotability

jokes.text: what's your favorite food to eat when you're disappointed?
jokes.setup: Marge trying to break bad news to Bart gently
jokes.punchline: Her tactful but obvious way of preparing for his disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - her gentle way of handling difficult conversations

jokes.text: Get real, ding-dong!
jokes.setup: Bart insisting he can be good
jokes.punchline: Lisa's blunt dismissal using childish insult
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling insult - fits Lisa's character but not original

jokes.text: Good one, ding-dong.
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately adopts and uses the insult
jokes.explanation: Quick callback showing Homer's childish side - good timing

jokes.text: Wherever that is.
jokes.setup: Bart promises to take his plate to the dishwasher
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't know where it is
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's genuine unfamiliarity with helping

jokes.text: I love Bart as much as you do, but actually not.
jokes.setup: Homer starts to express equal love for Bart
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer moment - perfect character integration showing his honesty and poor parenting

jokes.text: if you'd be so kind as to start my car for me
jokes.setup: Homer about to leave for Moe's
jokes.punchline: Asks Marge to start his car (implying it might explode or he's lazy)
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag setup but not fully developed

jokes.text: I buried all my whoopee cushions in the backyard.
jokes.setup: Bart explaining how he's avoiding temptation
jokes.punchline: The extreme measure of burying his prank supplies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's extreme commitment to being good

jokes.text: That's not where I buried them.
jokes.setup: Bart finds his whoopee cushions dug up
jokes.punchline: They've moved or someone else buried prank items there
jokes.explanation: Visual callback to the burial setup with mild absurdist payoff

jokes.text: Don't take my custom limo! At least let me sniff my coke out of the floor mats!
jokes.punchline: His desperate plea to keep drugs hidden in his limo
jokes.explanation: Dark humor fitting Krusty's degenerate character - adult humor for adult viewers

jokes.text: worse luck in the Bitcoin market
jokes.punchline: Reference to volatile cryptocurrency market
jokes.explanation: Topical reference but lacks depth - feels like easy contemporary humor

jokes.text: heavy investment in a high-end bookmark company
jokes.punchline: Investing heavily in bookmarks in the digital age
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist business idea that fits Krusty's poor judgment - original and well-crafted

jokes.text: That horse has left the stable, gone to the glue factory and has been used to make art projects.
jokes.setup: Responding to suggestion about going off air while still good
jokes.punchline: Extending the 'horse has left the stable' metaphor to absurd conclusion
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of tired metaphor to absurd logical conclusion - very quotable

jokes.text: Italian SpongeBob doing war commentary
jokes.punchline: SpongeBob characters dealing with WWII mine in Italian
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on foreign TV adaptations - SpongeBob in wartime is inherently funny

jokes.text: Not my Monet! I only looked at it once!
jokes.punchline: Claims he barely enjoyed his expensive art
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's regret and poor appreciation of culture

jokes.text: Not my Shetland dolphin! Although I won't miss the constant yapping.
jokes.punchline: Shetland dolphins don't exist, and dolphins don't yap like dogs
jokes.explanation: Absurdist animal that doesn't exist with wrong animal behavior - creative and memorable

jokes.text: Gum on the floor. Off the list.
jokes.setup: Beginning of Skinner's arbitrary rule enforcement
jokes.punchline: Immediate harsh punishment for minor infraction
jokes.explanation: Establishes the running gag of Skinner's harsh enforcement - good character consistency

jokes.text: Unauthorized meat-a-pult. Off the list!
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'meat-a-pult' for food catapult
jokes.explanation: Good made-up word that sounds official - builds the running gag effectively

jokes.text: Excuses are like handkerchiefs; everybody's got one. I don't have a handkerchief. Off the list!
jokes.punchline: Student's literal response backfires spectacularly
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of common saying with perfect logical trap - student walks into it beautifully

jokes.text: You're like egg salad at a picnic, Simpson. Even when you look good, we know you're going bad.
jokes.setup: Skinner commenting on Bart's apparent good behavior
jokes.punchline: Extended metaphor comparing Bart to spoiling food
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor that's both original and perfectly captures Skinner's view of Bart - highly quotable

jokes.text: I object to this arbitrary use of power and request I be taken off the list. Off the list! Thank you.
jokes.setup: Lisa formally objecting to Skinner's methods
jokes.punchline: She gets exactly what she asked for but not what she wanted
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - her principled stand backfires in exactly the way you'd expect

jokes.text: I'm getting strange red marks.
jokes.setup: Bart hasn't pranked anyone in five days
jokes.punchline: Physical withdrawal symptoms from not pranking
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy concept showing pranking as addiction - fits Bart's character well

jokes.text: Something's wrong with my long yellow car.
jokes.setup: School bus is clearly broken down
jokes.punchline: Otto's casual, understated description of the bus
jokes.explanation: Good Otto character moment - his stoner understatement of obvious problems

jokes.text: I've broken a ten-year-old's spirit. Time to celebrate with a fruit-on-the-bottom yogurt.
jokes.punchline: Celebrates with mundane, pathetic reward
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly capturing Skinner's petty sadism and sad personal life - excellent character work

jokes.text: Plain, plain, plain, plain... Mmm! Fruit!
jokes.punchline: His excitement over finding fruit at the bottom
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the yogurt celebration showing his pathetic pleasures

jokes.text: 'She heard me.'
jokes.setup: Skinner realizing Agnes heard his comment
jokes.punchline: His panicked realization
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that works for character dynamics

jokes.text: Simpson... has to go... on the... submarine.
jokes.punchline: His stilted speech pattern while making the request
jokes.explanation: Continues the impression but doesn't add much beyond the setup

jokes.text: You can put lipstick on a pig...
jokes.punchline: Uses actual Obama campaign quote
jokes.explanation: Just a reference to actual political quote - minimal comedy value

jokes.text: Hai-hai! Hu-hu! Heil-heil!
jokes.setup: Foreign clowns doing their versions of 'hey-hey'
jokes.punchline: One version sounds like Nazi greeting
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about foreign adaptations but treads into potentially offensive territory

jokes.text: Just do the Krusty show in your country and send me ten percent. Then send me another 60%. Then five percent more, and you're good.
jokes.punchline: Math adds up to 75% cut for him
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's greed and bad business sense

jokes.text: She's the reason I was able to purchase a new watch band. Always bragging about his watch band.
jokes.setup: Skinner crediting Suze Orman for financial success
jokes.punchline: His pathetic achievement and Bart's observation
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Skinner's small-scale financial 'success' and Bart's awareness

jokes.text: If only I could tell time.
jokes.punchline: Admits he can't read clocks
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and irony - having the accessory but not the skill to use it

jokes.text: Wanti wanti can't get it, getti getti no want it.
jokes.punchline: Authentic Jamaican saying in cartoon context
jokes.explanation: Cultural reference but doesn't add much comedy value beyond authenticity

jokes.text: They smoke, they toke, they smoke and toke and smoke
jokes.setup: Jamaican version of Itchy and Scratchy
jokes.punchline: Violence replaced with drug use
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural adaptation replacing cartoon violence with drug references

jokes.text: Me ma, she had 12 children, but only three lived. Then they closed the mill. Hey-hey.
jokes.punchline: Depressing Irish history delivered as children's entertainment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor contrasting Irish tragedy with children's show format - very effective

jokes.text: Fire that torpedo, Milhouse. Aye, aye. Where'd it go? It was just imaginary.
jokes.setup: Kids on submarine playing with controls
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic imaginary torpedo
jokes.explanation: Mild visual gag about kids' disappointment with submarine reality

jokes.text: As I always say, don't get mad; get dinner. Then get even.
jokes.punchline: Adds his own food-focused twist to common saying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - modifies wisdom to include food priority

jokes.text: Everything.
jokes.setup: Bart asks what Skinner's weakness is
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt, comprehensive assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character insight - Homer's brutal honesty in one word

jokes.text: I woke up and, surprisingly, Mother wasn't there beside me.
jokes.punchline: Casually reveals he sleeps with his mother
jokes.explanation: Classic Skinner mommy issues humor - perfectly in character but familiar territory

jokes.text: Well, I'm sure our town's police force will handle this competently.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's confident statement about his own incompetence
jokes.explanation: Standard Wiggum incompetence humor - fits character but predictable

jokes.text: There are some things only a boy should see.
jokes.setup: Homer about to help dispose of 'body'
jokes.punchline: Reversal of 'things children shouldn't see' trope
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of protective parenting trope - dark humor with good economy

jokes.text: Just call me The Cleaner.
jokes.setup: Homer taking charge of crime scene cleanup
jokes.punchline: Adopts crime movie nickname
jokes.explanation: Generic crime movie reference - fits Homer's fantasy but not particularly original

jokes.text: Look, you kill them, I get rid of them. That's always been our deal.
jokes.punchline: Implies this is their regular arrangement
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor suggesting Homer has done this before - unexpected character depth

jokes.text: Do you want the head? No, no, God, no. Well, if you change your mind, it'll be in my freezer. But only for a week. Got a lot of ice cream coming in.
jokes.punchline: Casual storage logistics mixed with ice cream delivery
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy mixing gruesome crime with mundane domestic concerns - very memorable

jokes.text: There never was a monkey. I was just tellin' stories to forget me consumption.
jokes.punchline: Reveals tragic backstory about tuberculosis
jokes.explanation: Good callback with dark Irish humor - consumption reference adds authenticity

jokes.text: What, you mean President-for-Life Krusty?
jokes.punchline: He's become a dictator
jokes.explanation: Good political satire about how entertainment figures become authoritarians

jokes.text: Now I know why Ma walked into the sea with her pockets full of rocks, on Christmas.
jokes.punchline: Casually references mother's suicide on Christmas
jokes.explanation: Consistent with Irish Krusty's tragic character - dark humor with good timing

jokes.text: I don't look anything like this 'Dick Fiddler.'
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous fake name
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny fake name but relies on innuendo - fits the crime parody

jokes.text: 'could I eat one of the potatoes?' 'Sorry, but Dick Fiddler is allergic to potatoes.'
jokes.punchline: Homer's commitment to fake identity includes dietary restrictions
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about method acting for fake identity

jokes.text: Dick Fiddler is allergic to potatoes.
jokes.setup: Skinner wants to eat a potato
jokes.punchline: Homer's made-up character detail
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist detail but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: what are you doing this Friday?
jokes.setup: Homer talking about faking his own death
jokes.punchline: Immediate timeline for the plan
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's impulsiveness about serious topics

jokes.text: I'm starting to think this 'school project' is more than meets the eye.
jokes.punchline: Agnes's massive understatement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Agnes character moment - understated reaction to dramatic situation

jokes.text: when this fat guy and his kid asked me to fake my death to punish you, I said, 'Sure, that's something to do.'
jokes.punchline: Her casual agreement to elaborate revenge scheme
jokes.explanation: Excellent Agnes character work - perfectly captures her boredom and meanness

jokes.text: I've stopped taking those pills that keep me nice.
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was medicated to be tolerable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark character revelation - explains Agnes's personality and threatens worse

jokes.text: I also can't ad-lib, memorize anything, improv or tell a joke.
jokes.setup: Krusty admitting he's bad in crisis
jokes.punchline: Lists all the skills a comedian should have that he lacks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty self-awareness - he's incompetent at his own profession

jokes.text: Well, I was supposed to be one of the sad ones.
jokes.setup: Question about why he became a clown
jokes.punchline: He was meant to be a sad/tragic clown but ended up in children's entertainment
jokes.explanation: Good character insight explaining Krusty's depression and poor performance

tmdb_overview: Principal Skinner promises the students that the best-behaved among them can take a ride in a submarine. When Skinner gives all the kids a clean slate, Bart believes even his own past indiscretions will be forgotten if he doesn’t get into any more trouble. Meanwhile Lisa tries to help a cash-poor Krusty to turn things around by suggesting he sell the foreign rights to his shows.
